topic: How AWS S3 Actually Stores a File

explainers.body: S3 is object storage, not a file system. Each upload is a flat key, no real folders.
Copies spread across many Availability Zones = your data survives disk failures.
Ask for the key and S3 returns the nearest healthy copy. Fast & consistent.

sticky1: Think of S3 as a giant key-value store!
sticky2: S3 stores objects, not files in folders!
quote_main: S3 is designed for 99.999999999% durability — that's 11 nines.
quote_sub: Lose 1 object? It'd take ~10 million years.
